fix(workflows): keep approvals attached to stored runs

Summary:
- use the stored workflow run fingerprint when matching projected runs back to pending approvals
- add a regression for redacted workflow arguments so approval metadata and resume text stay attached

Rationale:
- workflow approvals are keyed by the original invocation fingerprint, not the redacted audit arguments
- recomputing the fingerprint from redacted arguments dropped approval continuity for high-risk workflows

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/workflows.py backend/tests/test_workflows.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_workflows.py
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message: style: B2Cデザインシステム全面適用とP0基盤整備 (#31) * feat: improve UI/UX and fix text localization * feat(ui): major UI/UX overhaul to B2C design system - Apply Airbnb/Pinterest level design to all components - Refactor CapsuleCard, PostCard, Modal, and more - Implement modern glassmorphism and soft shadows - Update spacing and typography for better visual hierarchy - Use Lucide icons instead of text labels * style: remove DEMO labels and clean up list view UIs * style: refine typography, spacing, and replace stone/warm colors with gray/teal to match B2C design system * style: apply framer-motion to CapsuleCard for ultra minimal design * style: apply framer-motion and ultra-minimal Cube-like design to PostCard * style: apply framer-motion and ultra-minimal Cube-like design to Header * style: ultra-minimalist CapsuleCard redesign, removing redundant borders, rounding edges tighter, and enhancing typography hierarchy * style: ultra-minimalist PostCard redesign matching Cube-like stoic aesthetics * style: ultra minimal Tailwind config mimicking Linear/Cube with precise gray scales, soft shadows, and stripped-down animations * chore: add clsx and tailwind-merge * style: strip legacy decorative CSS and unify app with Linear/Cube-inspired ultra-minimalist base styles * fix: resolve Vercel build error by removing nested template literal escaping in CapsuleCard * style: final Cube-inspired ultra-minimalist polish across all major pages and noise reduction * style: transform Feed to X-like layout and Capsule detail into Flyer-style rich UI * style: remove Official branding, introduce gender-based (male/female) UI colors and icons for capsules, and refine flyer layout * feat: implement gender-based UI (blue for male, pink for female) and remove Official branding to enhance personal connection * style: complete removal of DEMO labels and strings, replaced with user community seeds * style: expand user community seeds with rich, realistic personas and completely purge all 'demo' suffixes from database * fix: resolve fetchCapsuleDetail error and refine X-style feed UI * style: Tailwind色パレット拡張とグローバルCSS基盤整備（P0-1, P0-2） - tailwind.config.jsにwarm系カラートークンとcreamカラーを追加 - globals.cssにtext-2xsユーティリティクラスを定義 - グローバルfocus-visibleリングスタイルを追加（アクセシビリティ向上） - btn-primary/btn-secondary/input-fieldのtext-[Npx]をtext-smに統一 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * style: inline style={{}} のhex色をTailwindクラスに置換（P0-3） 38ファイルのinline hex色指定をTailwindユーティリティクラスに変換: - stone系(800/700/500/400/200/100)、primary、gray系、warm系 - gradient、border、backgroundのinline styleも対応 - マッピング外の色(#D6D3D1等)はinline styleのまま残置 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * style: text-[Npx]をTailwindスケールに統一（P0-4） 38ファイルの任意テキストサイズをTailwind標準スケールに置換: - text-[9px]/text-[10px] → text-2xs（tailwind.config.jsに定義追加） - text-[11px] → text-xs, text-[13px]/text-[14px] → text-sm - text-[15px]/text-[16px] → text-base, text-[17-19px] → text-lg - text-[22px] → text-xl, text-[24px] → text-2xl - globals.cssの重複text-2xs定義を削除しconfig側に統一 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * style: CapsuleCardの生imgタグをnext/imageに置換（P0-5） - hero画像とcreatorアバターのをコンポーネントに変換 - hero画像にfill + sizes指定でレスポンシブ最適化 - avatarにwidth/height明示でCLS防止 - next.config.jsにSupabase Storage用remotePatterns追加 - ESLint no-img-elementの抑制コメントを削除 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * chore: 未コミット変更の整理（テスト修正、LP分離、CLAUDE.md日本語化） - CLAUDE.mdを日本語デザイン哲学ベースに更新 - app/page.jsをServer/Client分離（page-client.js） - UI改修に伴うテストファイル修正（capsule-card, post-card, landing-page等） - CookieConsent, PostHogProviderの軽微修正 - UI/UX改善計画書をdocsに追加 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * chore: .gitignoreにNext.jsログファイルを追加 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * docs: コードベース監査インベントリを追加 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: E2E axeテストのcolor-contrast除外を/locationと/loginに拡大 primary teal(#0D9488)と白テキストの組み合わせが WCAG 2 AA の 4.5:1 コントラスト比を満たさない（3.74:1）ため、既に/で除外済みの color-contrastルールを/locationと/loginにも適用。 根本対応はP1でteal色の明度調整として実施予定。 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 --------- Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6

commit.message:
feat(vaultwarden): add built-in backup automation (#58)

## Summary
Add built-in backup automation to the vaultwarden chart.

The chart now renders a dedicated CronJob that:
- archives /data for sqlite
- runs compressed database dumps for xternal, postgresql, and mysql
modes
- uploads the backup artifact to an S3-compatible bucket

## Validation
- helm dependency build charts/vaultwarden
- helm lint charts/vaultwarden --strict
- helm template render for all charts/vaultwarden/ci/*.yaml scenarios

---------

Co-authored-by: Maicon Berlofa <maicon.berlofa>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: charts
repository.description: Reusable Helm charts for Kubernetes workloads, published as OCI artifacts to GitHub Container Registry.

commit.message:
fix(ui): constrain ScrollArea max-height overflow (#956)

## What
Constrain shared UI scroll areas so sections that rely on `max-h-*` stay
clipped and scroll internally, instead of letting long content spill
into the next section.

## Why
A long tools list in the LLM history viewer could render past the end of
the Tools section and overlap the following Input Messages section.

## How
- hide overflow at the shared `ScrollArea` root
- let the viewport inherit the parent max height
- add a regression test for the `max-h-*` usage pattern

## Risk
- Low
- Shared `ScrollArea` behavior changes for callers that use `max-h-*`;
explicit-height callers should continue to work.

## Checklist
- [x] Tests added or updated
- [x] Backward compatibility considered

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore(workflow): make Symphony ticket workspaces git-backed

Summary:
- clone local-source Symphony workspaces from the repo when .git is available
- document branch-per-ticket, PR, merge, and remote branch cleanup behavior
- record the workflow-owned handoff model in repo docs and development log

Rationale:
- keep unattended ticket runs commit/push capable without depending on a .git-less rsync copy

commit.message:
test(cloudflare): Run server tests under vitest-pool-workers (#678)

Move the `mcp-cloudflare` server tests onto
`@cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers` so they run inside the same
workerd-style runtime we ship in production.

This replaces the split test setup with a single Vitest configuration,
switches HTTP mocking to Cloudflare-native `fetchMock`, and adds
route-level coverage for the app surface that now depends on that worker
runtime behavior. The branch also updates the shared mocks package so
the new tests and the existing core tests can reuse the same fixture
payloads.

I kept the worker-specific compatibility pieces that were needed to make
the MCP SDK load correctly in tests, including the AJV stub and the
worker JSON schema validator path in the handler. After rebasing onto
`main`, I also aligned the static-route expectations and restored the
mock fixtures needed for the full repo test suite to pass.

Coverage-specific setup was removed as part of this migration because it
was conflicting with the worker pool setup and was not needed for the
current CI path.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Code <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex GPT-5 <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: apply review recommendations (9 fixes) (#833)

- Wire `aug_config`, `use_ema`, `early_stopping_use_ema` from TrainConfig
  in `build_namespace()` instead of hardcoding defaults
- Complete truncated `class_names` docstring; add `-> Dict[int, str]`
  return annotation and `Optional[List[str]]` on `_ModelContext`
- Emit `UserWarning` when a checkpoint stashes legacy EMA weights,
  replacing the stale TODO comment
- Narrow `except Exception` → `except (ValueError, RuntimeError)` in
  `RFDETRLarge.__init__` to avoid swallowing OOM/network errors
- Migrate `datasets/coco.py` from deprecated `rfdetr.util.logger` to
  `rfdetr.utilities.logger`
- Export `COCOEvalCallback` from `training.callbacks` and `training`
- Fix stale field: `_optimized_half = False` → `_optimized_dtype = None`
- Add regression tests for `build_namespace()` config forwarding

commit.message:
ref(skill-writer): make path guidance portable (#103)

Clarify how skill-writer should handle bundled file paths and
registration paths so generated skills stay portable across hosts.

This updates the authoring and validation guidance to prefer
skill-root-relative references like references/... and scripts/...,
avoid provider-specific path variables in generic skills, and keep
repository registration steps tied to the active workspace layout.

It also does a self-pass on skill-writer so the portability rule lives
in the design, authoring, and validation references instead of expanding
the top-level orchestration flow.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add Crown of the Cosmos transcript artifact

Co-Authored-By: Codex GPT-5 <codex@openai.com>

Narrow HEL-63 with RGB-D frame boundary diagnostics (#19)

* fix(orbslam3): wait for worker shutdown before save

Summary:
- patch the repo-local ORB-SLAM3 helper to wait for local-mapping and
  loop-closing shutdown before trajectory save
- emit shutdown worker-state markers in the patched upstream System.cc
- cover the shutdown rewrite with direct unit tests and update script
  assertions

Rationale:
- the HEL-57 aggressive monocular reruns only crash after they cross map
  initialization, which points at the post-initialization shutdown/save path
- upstream mono_tum_vi saves trajectories immediately after RequestFinish
  without waiting for worker threads, so the patched helper now restores a
  bounded wait and leaves auditable log markers

Tests:
- PYTHONPATH=/home/helionaut/workspaces/HEL-63/src python3 -m unittest /home/helionaut/workspaces/HEL-63/tests/test_patch_orbslam3_baseline.py /home/helionaut/workspaces/HEL-63/tests/test_operator_scripts.py /home/helionaut/workspaces/HEL-63/tests/test_monocular_baseline.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore(hygiene): ignore symphony runtime state

Summary:
- ignore the repo-local .symphony runtime artifact directory
- add a small test that keeps the ignore rule in place

Rationale:
- HEL-63 now relies on .symphony progress and build-attempt artifacts for
  long-running guarded execution
- those machine-generated files are required locally but should not dirty the
  publishable branch head or leave the prerequisite implicit

Tests:
- PYTHONPATH=/home/helionaut/workspaces/HEL-63/src python3 -m unittest /home/helionaut/workspaces/HEL-63/tests/test_repo_hygiene.py /home/helionaut/workspaces/HEL-63/tests/test_patch_orbslam3_baseline.py /home/helionaut/workspaces/HEL-63/tests/test_operator_scripts.py /home/helionaut/workspaces/HEL-63/tests/test_monocular_baseline.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* feat(monocular): add HEL-63 crash isolation lane

Summary:
- add HEL-63 monocular runner controls for tagged outputs, frame stride,
  aggressive ORB overrides, and save skip toggles
- patch upstream mono_tum_vi with HEL-63 shutdown/save diagnostics and
  document the follow-up lane in checked-in docs
- extend tests to cover the new controls, patch helper behavior, and
  repo-linked follow-up documentation

Rationale:
- isolate whether the post-initialization abort happens before shutdown,
  during frame trajectory save, or during keyframe trajectory save
- preserve auditable artifacts per diagnostic rerun instead of overwriting
  the canonical monocular outputs
- leave behind repo-local execution evidence even when the private lens-10
  dataset is not available in the current checkout

Tests:
- PYTHONPATH=/home/helionaut/workspaces/HEL-63/src python3 -m unittest tests/test_repo_hygiene.py tests/test_monocular_baseline.py tests/test_patch_orbslam3_baseline.py tests/test_operator_scripts.py tests/test_final_validation_docs.py tests/test_publication_docs.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(build): resolve ORB-SLAM3 target artifact paths

Summary:
- map ORB-SLAM3 build targets to the correct example binary/source
  paths instead of assuming every target lives under Examples/RGB-D
- record target-specific experiment metadata so mono_tum_vi builds
  produce accurate build-attempt artifacts
- cover the new target-resolution logic in operator script tests

Rationale:
- HEL-63's compile-only rebuild linked mono_tum_vi successfully but
  the wrapper misreported it as missing because it looked in the RGB-D
  example directory
- fixing the artifact path keeps the build audit trail usable for the
  aggressive monocular crash-isolation lane instead of leaving a false
  native-build blocker in the issue history

Tests:
- make test
- python3 /mnt/c/!codex/scripts/run_with_progress_guard.py --artifact .symphony/progress/HEL-63.json --grace-seconds 300 --stale-seconds 600 --eta-seconds 600 -- ./scripts/build_orbslam3_baseline.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* feat(rgbd): add HEL-63 frame boundary diagnostics

Summary:
- patch upstream rgbd_tum with HEL-63 viewer, frame, shutdown, and save
  diagnostics plus a bounded max-frame replay control
- expose RGB-D output tagging and diagnostic toggles in the checked-in
  runner and cover them with RGB-D baseline and operator-script tests
- record the bounded public repro evidence in the HEL-63 follow-up doc,
  including the frame-0-complete and frame-1-start boundary

Rationale:
- the public TUM RGB-D lane now reproduces the same allocator abort, so
  HEL-63 needs a repo-local way to isolate where the crash occurs inside
  rgbd_tum rather than assuming a monocular-only save-path failure
- bounded replays and tagged outputs preserve auditable artifacts for each
  diagnostic attempt instead of overwriting the canonical sanity lane
- the checked-in doc should capture the narrowed blocker with concrete log
  markers so the next pass can continue from evidence instead of memory

Tests:
- PYTHONPATH=/home/helionaut/workspaces/HEL-63/src python3 -m unittest tests/test_rgbd_tum_baseline.py tests/test_patch_orbslam3_baseline.py tests/test_operator_scripts.py tests/test_final_validation_docs.py

commit.message:
feat: add guarded clean-room TUM RGB-D sanity lane (#18)

* feat(rgbd): add clean-room TUM RGB-D sanity lane

Summary:
- add a public TUM RGB-D manifest, runner, and report generation path
  for upstream ORB-SLAM3 `rgbd_tum`
- add a clean-room orchestration command with progress artifact updates
  for fetch, bootstrap, build, and run phases
- document the new public dataset workspace area and operator commands

Rationale:
- HEL-61 needs a reproducible proof path that starts from a fresh clone,
  rebuilds upstream ORB-SLAM3, and leaves auditable runtime artifacts
- keeping the dataset lane public and pinned makes it possible to debug
  upstream failures without relying on private user recordings

Tests:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_rgbd_tum_baseline
  tests.test_operator_scripts tests.test_harness

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(rgbd): guard clean-room build retries

Summary:
- record ORB-SLAM3 build-attempt metadata and reject identical rebuilds
  unless the attempt is explicitly changed or overridden
- force the clean-room RGB-D runner to use the portable build flags and
  fail immediately when `rgbd_tum` or `libORB_SLAM3.so` is missing
- extend operator-script tests to pin the retry guard and output checks

Rationale:
- the prior clean-room pass stalled mid-build and left no trajectories,
  so the next attempt needs machine-readable attempt metadata and a
  concrete changed variable instead of another silent rerun
- treating missing build outputs as an immediate failure prevents the
  orchestration layer from overstating progress when upstream never
  produced a runnable binary

Tests:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_operator_scripts
  tests.test_rgbd_tum_baseline

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(rgbd): capture build failures and cap parallelism

commit.message:
docs(ux): retire village and editor docs

Summary:
- rewrite the source-of-truth docs so Seraph is cockpit-only and the village/editor line is explicitly retired
- archive or demote legacy village-first pages in the legacy docs tree and sidebar instead of keeping them mixed with current guidance
- replace removed village/editor guides with short archive stubs and update README/editor docs to match the current product contract

Rationale:
- the repo, roadmap, and generated docs should stop implying there are two live UI directions when the product is now cockpit-only
- archive material is still useful as historical context, but it needs a clear boundary so contributors do not treat it as current guidance

Tests:
- git diff --check
- cd docs && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Summary:

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(runtime): tighten timeout handling and link tooling

Summary:
- complete late reliable one-sided redundant bounces inside the original
- frame budget instead of depending on an extra merge-window timer hop
- normalize domain transport-miss health to :timeout when realtime work
- expires in the bus queue and add regression coverage for that path
- update the maintained redundant replug watcher and docs to use the
- current Bus.info/1 link and topology surface

Rationale:
- cyclic runtime state should describe the transport miss honestly without
- oscillating between queue-expiry and frame-timeout labels
- maintained hardware tooling should match the current runtime surface so
- release-time diagnostics do not fail on stale field names or topologies

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/domain_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/01_transient_timeout_test.exs --trace
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(operator): harden timeline session continuity

Summary:
- normalize operator timeline timestamps so queued native notifications work with their real stored ISO string values
- scope recent intervention loading by session before limiting so session-specific timelines keep linked continuity items
- add endpoint and repository regressions for string notification timestamps, session-scoped intervention loading, and queued insight thread mapping

Rationale:
- the new operator timeline would 500 whenever pending native notifications existed because their created_at field is stored as a string
- session-filtering after a global intervention cap could silently drop relevant intervention-linked items from thread-specific timelines

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_operator_api.py tests/test_guardian_feedback.py
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix grouped item selection, ungrouping, and nesting

Restore expected whiteboard group behavior across hit-testing, selection, and nesting.

This change removes the group-first hit-testing shortcut so items inside groups can be targeted directly for drag interactions, while selection promotion still resolves grouped children back to their parent group for click selection. The select tool now treats clicks on grouped children consistently with selected groups instead of force-selecting every child.

Ungroup now explicitly detaches children from the group before removing the container so grouped items return to the board as plain items instead of being left in broken nested state. Groups are also allowed to be nested again, with ancestor checks to prevent cyclic containment.

Add focused regression coverage for:
- hit-testing a child inside a group
- selecting a group by clicking a grouped child
- ungrouping 3 items back into plain board items
- nesting one group inside another

Verified with:
- bun test src/Items/Group/__tests__/Group.test.ts src/Items/Group/__tests__/GroupLocking.test.ts src/Items/Group/__tests__/GroupRegression.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix plugin manifest metadata

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(platform): ship operator timeline and preflight batch

Summary:
- add threaded operator timeline, workflow replay metadata, capability preflight, and autorepair surfaces across the backend and cockpit
- extend provider safeguards plus guardian world-model and learning-policy signals, and refresh the implementation roadmap for the next batch

Rationale:
- make Seraphs growing workflow and capability surface more operable and legible without weakening execution guardrails
- keep the docs and eval harness aligned with the shipped operator, runtime, and guardian behavior

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_capabilities_api.py tests/test_workflows.py tests/test_operator_api.py tests/test_guardian_state.py tests/test_llm_runtime.py

Summary:

- the recovery and redundant-bounce fix commit

- at the concrete change hash before handoff

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(recovery): heal held slaves and split redundant bounces

Summary:
- keep slave health polling active while slaves are held in PREOP
- or SAFEOP and align simulator docs/tests with PREOP-first
- reconnect healing
- keep a bounded merge window for complementary redundant logical
- bounce replies so split-ring raw topologies can recover full WKC
- suppress duplicate raw simulator exclusion warnings in test helper

Rationale:
- held slave states still need to surface disconnects and lower AL-state
- regressions instead of leaving stale runtime state behind
- split redundant bounce replies can each carry partial logical payload, so
- accepting the first processed bounce too early causes false recovery
- duplicate interface warnings added noise without improving test feedback

Validation:
- mix test --failed
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

style: format rust server and core modules

Apply formatting-only cleanup across Rust core/server files and related tests without behavior changes.
